campaign.title: Improve on ASAL: life-cycle-aware search for artificial life
campaign.problem:
# Improve on ASAL: life-cycle-aware search for artificial life

## Problem Statement

ASAL (Kumar et al. 2024, arXiv:2412.17799) reframed artificial-life search as
foundation-model-guided optimization: embed a simulation's rollout frames in a
frozen vision-language model (CLIP ViT-B/32), then optimize one of three scalar
objectives — **supervised target** (match a text prompt), **open-endedness**
(trajectory never repeats in FM space), or **illumination** (population-level
diversity) — with Sep-CMA-ES or a custom novelty archive. ASAL covers Boids,
Lenia, Particle Life, Particle Lenia, NCA, and Game of Life.

We aim to **improve on ASAL along three orthogonal axes** and produce results +
visualizations of the same character (species atlases, life-cycle GIFs,
illumination maps, cross-method comparisons):

1. **Substrate gap:** add Flow-Lenia (Plantec et al. 2023, arXiv:2212.07906),
   the mass-conserving Lenia variant that uniquely enables multi-species
   coexistence, soft self-boundaries, and intrinsic evolution within one world.
   ASAL did not cover Flow-Lenia.

2. **Foundation-model gap:** ASAL uses per-frame CLIP ViT-B/32. Investigate
   whether stronger image FMs (DINOv2, SigLIP-2) or temporal FMs (V-JEPA,
   VideoMAE, InternVideo2) yield a richer search signal — especially for
   behaviors whose essence is temporal (motion, development, reproduction).

3. **Objective gap:** ASAL's open-endedness rewards any trajectory that keeps
   moving in FM space — including chaotic noise. Replace with **life-cycle
   fidelity:** given a fixed bank of ordered prompt-sequences describing life
   stages (e.g. *"seed" → "developing organism" → "mature self-sustaining
   creature" → "reproducing creature"*), optimize for simulations whose rollout
   matches the ordered trajectory under the chosen FM. Structured novelty, not
   any novelty.

The research output is both quantitative (single-scalar leaderboard metric, see
below) and qualitative (GIFs, species atlases, perturbation-recovery videos,
multi-species ecology visualizations, cross-substrate comparisons).

## Solution Interface

A candidate "solution" is a search procedure that, given a fixed substrate
(Lenia / Flow-Lenia / Particle Lenia / NCA) and fixed FM, discovers a set of
simulation parameters whose rollouts score well on the life-cycle fidelity
metric.

Concretely, a solution is a Python module implementing:

```python
def search(substrate_name: str,
           fm_name: str,
           prompt_bank: list[list[str]],   # life-cycle prompt sequences
           budget: dict,                    # {"generations": int, "pop_size": int}
           rng: jax.Array) -> dict:
    """
    Returns:
      {
        "best_params": jax.Array,          # flat params of best simulation
        "best_rollout": jax.Array,         # [T, H, W, C] frames of best
        "archive": list[dict],             # top-K solutions for illumination
        "trace": dict,                     # per-generation metric curves
      }
    """
```

Candidates may use any search algorithm (Sep-CMA-ES baseline, custom novelty
archive à la ASAL, QDax MAP-Elites, AURORA, hierarchical QD, learned proposal
distributions, etc.). They must consume the same substrate and FM interfaces
(adapted from the ASAL repo; Flow-Lenia adapter to be written in Phase 2).

## Success Metric

**Life-cycle fidelity score** (single scalar, direction = maximize).

On a fixed bank of K life-cycle prompt sequences (e.g. 5 sequences × 5 prompts
each), for each sequence:
1. Run the discovered simulation for T steps, sample 5 equally-spaced frames.
2. Embed frames with the chosen FM → `z_i`, embed prompts → `z_txt_i`.
3. Compute **softmax-target score** (ASAL's collapse-resistant variant):
   symmetric cross-entropy on `softmax(z_txt @ z_img.T / τ)`.

Final score = mean over K sequences, minus an ASAL-OE baseline computed on a
held-out seed pool to anchor zero. Range: roughly `[0, 1]` in practice;
direction `maximize`.

**Why not use raw ASAL-OE or supervised-target score alone:** raw OE is gamed
by chaotic trajectories; raw supervised-target (ASAL's existing metric) only
scores static matching, not ordered temporal trajectories. Life-cycle fidelity
is the minimal extension that (a) stays a single differentiable scalar, (b) is
evaluable from the raw rollout tensor with a frozen FM, and (c) rewards
structured temporal behavior.

**Target:** no hard target set; this is an exploration-first campaign. We will
measure improvement relative to an ASAL-exact baseline (Sep-CMA-ES with CLIP
ViT-B/32 optimizing supervised target, scored on our life-cycle prompts).

## Known Approaches

Starting points to inherit / adapt / beat:

- **ASAL** (arXiv:2412.17799, github.com/SakanaAI/asal): JAX-native substrates,
  `asal_metrics.py` (60 LOC, directly reusable), Sep-CMA-ES via evosax, custom
  novelty-archive GA. This is the baseline to beat.
- **Flow-Lenia** (arXiv:2212.07906, github.com/erwanplantec/FlowLenia): JAX +
  Equinox. Needs a ~200-LOC adapter to plug into the ASAL substrate interface.
- **Leniabreeder** (Faldor & Cully, ALIFE 2024): QDax-based Lenia + MAP-Elites
  + AURORA. The canonical QD-for-Lenia reference.
- **QDax** (github.com/adaptive-intelligent-robotics/QDax): JAX-native QD
  library, actively maintained.
- **Foundation models:** HuggingFace `FlaxCLIPModel` (CLIP), `FlaxDinov2Model`
  (DINOv2). SigLIP via big_vision (weekend of integration work). V-JEPA /
  VideoMAE require PyTorch + JAX interop.
- **Classic metrics survey:** Bedau–Packard evolutionary activity, Channon's
  unbounded-evolution criteria, MODES toolbox — all require genealogy
  instrumentation; deferred to later campaigns.

## Visualization Targets

The campaign outputs are as much the figures as the metric. Required per
promising orbit:

- **Species atlas GIF:** grid of discovered patterns running in parallel.
- **Life-cycle GIF:** single pattern from seed through mature dynamics.
- **Perturbation-recovery GIF:** pattern → damage → regeneration timeline.
- **Multi-species ecology GIF** (Flow-Lenia only): multiple patterns
  interacting in a shared world.
- **Illumination map:** 2D projection of discovered parameter space, colored
  by FM-embedding descriptor.
- **Comparison GIF:** same initial conditions across substrates (Lenia vs.
  Flow-Lenia vs. NCA) or across FMs (CLIP vs. DINOv2 vs. V-JEPA).

## Open Questions (to be resolved by Phase 1.5 refinement panel)

1. Exact formula for life-cycle fidelity: softmax-target only, or combined
   with an OE penalty? How to weight?
2. Fixed prompt bank vs. evolving prompt bank? Human-written vs. LLM-generated?
3. What counts as the "held-out ASAL baseline" anchor — is it a fixed score
   frozen at init, or re-computed per orbit?
4. Unit of comparison across orbits: per-generation budget equalized, or
   wall-clock equalized, or GPU-hour equalized?
5. Does a single-scalar life-cycle score suffice, or do we need a
   multi-objective Pareto front (e.g. fidelity × robustness × diversity)?

eval_methodology.summary:
> Frozen at eval-v1. Describes WHAT the evaluator measures and HOW —
> the research contract that all orbits must satisfy.
> For the full formal spec, see `problem_spec.md`. For the judge rubric,
> see `judge/rubric.md`.
stages: **Existence** — is there a coherent localized pattern?
**Agency** — does it move or deform in a structured way?
**Robustness** — does it persist with recognizable identity?
**Reproduction** — does it bud, divide, or produce offspring?
search container — produce per-seed frame strips:

orbits.name: 01-asal-lifecycle-prompts
orbits.strategy: 01-asal-lifecycle-prompts: Sep-CMA-ES + ASAL supervised-target on ordered life-cycle prompts (seed->growing->mature->reproducing)

issue_comments.author: wwang2
issue_comments.body:
<!-- RE:CRITIC -->
```json
{
  "verdict": "REVISE",
  "overall_comment": "narrative is readable but metric value is not annotated, behavior.gif is below the 512x512 / 500KB minimum, and results.png contains debug-leak text ('illustrative search trace / actual Modal run populates ...') plus no cross-seed error bars.",
  "figures": [
    {
      "path": "orbits/01-asal-lifecycle-prompts/figures/narrative.png",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "measured metric value (final best_proxy score, e.g. -2.22) is nowhere on the figure; only the loss equation is printed. A reader cannot tell what number this method achieved.", "fix": "add a bold annotation in the top-right or under the title like 'best inner-loop score = -2.22 (ours) vs -2.74 (ASAL baseline), gap = 0.52' using the final measured metric passed by the orbit."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "t=127, t=191, t=255 panels look visually near-identical (uniform orb tiling) which undermines the life-cycle narrative promising 'budding offspring' and 'two creatures side by side'.", "fix": "either crop each panel to a smaller spatial ROI that actually shows the morphological transition, or overlay arrows / circles highlighting the specific region where division is visible; if the rollout genuinely does not differentiate these phases, drop to 3 prompts instead of 5 so the story is honest."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "prompt captions in small grey text below each orange t-label are readable but lack contrast against white; they also wrap awkwardly mid-phrase.", "fix": "bump caption font to 12pt, set color to near-black #0a0a0a, and force single-line layout by widening panel gutters or shortening the prompts to <=40 chars."}
      ]
    },
    {
      "path": "orbits/01-asal-lifecycle-prompts/figures/behavior.gif",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "resolution is 384x384, below the 512x512 floor; file is 186KB, below the 500KB floor — the GIF will look blurry when embedded on GitHub Pages.", "fix": "re-render with Image.NEAREST upscaling to 512x512 (or native-render at 512) and keep 10-15 frames over a clean loop; target 500KB-2MB."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "poster frame (t=000) is 90% empty purple background with a tiny seed dot — weak thumbnail for a reader scrolling the Issue.", "fix": "set the GIF's first frame to the most compelling mid-rollout frame (e.g. t=127) OR prepend a 6-frame 'title card' with the orbit name + metric value before the animation loops."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "'t=000' label is ~8pt in bottom-left corner and barely legible.", "fix": "increase step-counter to 14pt white text with a black outline, and move it to top-left with a semi-transparent black pill behind it."}
      ]
    },
    {
      "path": "orbits/01-asal-lifecycle-prompts/figures/results.png",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "subtitle on panel (b) reads 'illustrative search trace / actual Modal run populates search_trace.best_proxy_per_gen' — this is dev debug text that looks like a placeholder and undermines credibility. A reader cannot tell if the curve is real or mocked.", "fix": "remove the 'illustrative' caveat entirely. If the data IS from the Modal run, title it 'Sep-CMA-ES convergence (60 generations, actual Modal run, seed=0)'. If it is NOT real, the figure cannot ship — regenerate from real search_trace.best_proxy_per_gen."},
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "no error bars across seeds on either panel — the shaded band around panel (b) appears to be a smoothing envelope, not seed variance, so the 'final gap = 0.52' claim is unsupported.", "fix": "run >=3 seeds (or report the existing seeds if multiple were run) and plot mean plus/minus std as the shaded band; annotate 'n=3 seeds' in the legend."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "panel (a) shows frame variance + frame mean vs rollout step, which is tangentially related to the metric and is the larger of the two panels — it buries the actual results in panel (b).", "fix": "swap the panel sizes so (b) convergence is the wide panel (2/3 width) and (a) pixel-stats is the narrow sidebar (1/3 width); or drop panel (a) entirely and replace with a bar showing final metric +/- std across seeds for ours vs baseline."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "top titles for (a) and (b) overlap visually with the suptitle 'Orbit 01 ...' because they sit on the same horizontal band.", "fix": "add fig.subplots_adjust(top=0.82) and drop the per-panel titles down 8pt, or move the suptitle to a separate line above with y=0.98."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "final measured metric value is not printed as a large standalone number anywhere on results.png.", "fix": "add a text box in panel (b) upper-left: 'ours: -2.22 | baseline: -2.74 | gap: 0.52' in 14pt monospace."}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

If OOM recurs at gen>10, fall back to pop_size=16 + checkpoint-resume rather than shipping empty figures."}

```json
{
  "verdict": "REVISE",
  "overall_comment": "results.png is a self-labeled illustrative placeholder (not real data), behavior.gif poster frame shows an empty mass-conservation plot, and no figure displays the final CLIP-OE metric — frontmatter metric is still null so results panel cannot be honest yet.",

    {
      "path": "orbits/03-flow-lenia-clip-oe/figures/narrative.png",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "No metric value is annotated on the frame strip. A reader cannot tell whether flow-lenia 'wins' — baseline and method panels look qualitatively similar at t=255.", "fix": "Add the final CLIP-OE (LCF) score as a row-level annotation next to each strategy label (e.g. 'flow-lenia  μ=0.17 α=1.3  —  LCF=0.326'). If the Modal metric is still pending, at minimum show the best CLIP-OE score each row achieved during search."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Pure-red colormap is not in the style guide's approved list (viridis/magma/inferno/cividis). Red-on-black reads as a single-channel debug view, not a narrative figure.", "fix": "Render the mass channel with cmap='magma' or 'inferno' on black background — this is what the schematic already uses and keeps the set visually consistent."},

This is a placeholder, not measured data — shipping it would mislead readers of the Issue and Pages site.", "fix": "Either (a) wait until the Modal search_trace lands and re-render with the real 4-restart curves, or (b) drop results.png entirely until real data exists. Never publish a figure whose own title admits it is fake."},
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "Right-panel bar chart shows per-restart 'final CLIP-OE' but no baseline bar, no error bars / seed variance, and no reference to the judge's LCF score — the campaign metric. A reader has no way to tell whether 0.326 is good.", "fix": "Add a baseline bar (plain Lenia + Sep-CMA-ES at σ=0.15) alongside the σ-schedule restarts, add ±1σ error bars across ≥3 seeds, and annotate the best bar with the geometric-mean LCF it produced (or note 'LCF pending')."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Default matplotlib tab colors (blue/orange/green/red) instead of the style guide's seaborn-muted palette. Also no grid / spine styling per style.md.", "fix": "Apply the rcParams block from research/style.md and use COLORS={'σ=0.15':'#4C72B0','σ=0.08':'#DD8452','σ=0.25':'#55A868','σ=0.12':'#C44E52'} consistently across both panels."}
      ]

      "path": "orbits/03-flow-lenia-clip-oe/figures/behavior.gif",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "First frame (the GitHub poster frame shown before the GIF plays) displays an EMPTY mass-conservation line plot — axes 0–500, no lines drawn, legend entries 'baseline'/'method' point at nothing. Readers scanning the Issue will see broken content.", "fix": "Either (a) render the mass trace fully before frame 0 so the first frame shows the complete ΣA curve with a moving marker, or (b) make frame 0 a title card with orbit name, metric, and the last frame's t=255 snapshot. The poster frame is the most-seen asset — it must stand alone."},
        {"severity": "MAJOR", "issue": "The qualitative panels at frame 0 are indistinguishable random-noise discs with identical 'mass=479.3' — nothing in the first frame tells the story of what flow-lenia does differently from baseline.", "fix": "Start the GIF at a later frame (e.g. t=60) where the pattern has emerged, OR add a persistent LCF/CLIP-OE annotation in each panel title that updates as the rollout progresses."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "1900×720 is wide but each substrate panel is only ~700px tall — the right-side mass plot eats half the horizontal budget for a 1D trace.", "fix": "Move the mass-conservation trace to a narrow strip (e.g. 1900×180) above or below the two substrate panels so each substrate gets a larger square canvas."},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "At 10 fps × 26 frames the GIF is 2.6 s — on the short side for showing a 256-step rollout; pacing feels skimmed.", "fix": "Either sample 40–50 frames at 12 fps (~3.5 s) or show the last-half of the rollout only (t=128–256) to emphasise the persistent-structure regime."}
      ]
    },
    {
      "path": "orbits/03-flow-lenia-clip-oe/figures/schematic.png",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Arrows + equations are clean, but there is no axes/scale reference and the blue conservation arc visually overlaps the 'kernel convolution' and 'semi-Lagrangian transport' subcaptions at narrow viewport widths.", "fix": "Lift the blue arc 10–15% higher so it clears the subcaption row, and add a small 'ΣA = const' label at the arc apex so the invariant is self-documenting even if the subtitle scrolls off."}
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

issue_comments.author: reviewer
issue_comments.body:
<!-- RE:REVIEW orbit=03-flow-lenia-clip-oe -->
**orbit-reviewer:** orbit/03-flow-lenia-clip-oe — Advisory Notes

---

## Code Quality

**[CODE NOTE]** The module docstring says "2-4 restarts with varying σ_init" but the code hardcodes exactly 4 restarts via `_RESTART_SIGMAS = (0.15, 0.08, 0.25, 0.12)` — there is no logic to produce 2 or 3. The log.md correctly says 4 restarts; the docstring is the mismatch.

**[CODE NOTE]** Inside the restart-budget block, the inline comment reads "Give half of remaining to this one, keep half for later" but the code uses `restart_budget = time_remaining * 0.55`. The log.md correctly documents 55%; the comment is stale and should say 55%.

**[CODE NOTE]** `search()` accepts `substrate_name: Literal["lenia", "flow_lenia"]` but never reads or validates it. If the evaluator passes `"lenia"`, the solution still silently runs Flow-Lenia physics (imported from evaluator). This is a latent correctness hazard — if the harness ever calls this with `substrate_name="lenia"` the wrong physics runs with no warning.

**[CODE NOTE]** Inner-loop fitness uses 4 CLIP frames at `{0, 63, 127, 255}` (_CLIP_FRAME_IDX); the judge eval strip uses 5 frames at `{0, 63, 127, 191, 255}`. The mismatch is intentional (lighter inner loop) but is not called out anywhere in log.md — a reader may assume they are identical. Worth one sentence noting the frame-set difference.

**[CODE NOTE]** `_oe_score` drops the first row of the pairwise cosine-similarity matrix via `jnp.tril(_, k=-1)`, which gives only *lower-triangular* pairs. With K=4 frames that yields 6 valid pairs, but the comment says "max off-diagonal … averaged across K=4 frames drawn from …" — the per-row max then mean is the correct ASAL-OE formula, and the masking is correct, but the comment is ambiguous about whether this is ASAL's exact formula vs a variant.

---

## Significance

**metric: null** — no Modal eval run has completed. The frontmatter records `metric: null` and Issue #4 has no `<!-- RE:EVAL -->` comment. Significance cannot be assessed.

```
baseline_lcf_judge = 0.1185  (eval-v1, Sep-CMA-ES+CLIP-OE on Lenia, 16 seeds)
orbit metric       = null    (pending Modal dispatch)
threshold          = not defined in config — protocol says METRIC > 0 at Wilcoxon p < 0.05
```

**[REPRODUCIBILITY]** `metric: null` in log.md frontmatter — no Modal run recorded. The orbit is in a pre-eval state; there is no verified metric to review.

**[REPRODUCIBILITY]** No `RE_RUN_ID` or Modal job ID recorded in log.md — if the search container runs, the checkpoint path `/tmp/ckpt/{run_id}_best.npy` cannot be reproduced without this identifier.

**[REPRODUCIBILITY]** References section notes "Added to `research/references/registry.yaml` (best-effort; this orbit does not modify the registry)" — the registry file does not exist in the repo. This is a known gap but should be noted.

---

## Figure Audit

### narrative.png



**[FIGURE ISSUE] narrative.png: no panel labels (a)(b)(c)** — the 2×5 grid has no `(a)`/`(b)` labels on the row panels. The style guide requires panel labels for comparison grids. → suggest: add `ax.text(-0.12, 1.05, '(a)', ...)` on the first cell of each row.

**[FIGURE ISSUE] narrative.png: log.md caption says "method row shows tighter soliton structure and more isolated blobs than the baseline's amoeboid mass"** — visually, at t=255 the baseline pattern has dispersed near the edge of the grid (top-right cell is nearly empty), while the method row shows a compact but amorphous blob. The caption's claim of "tighter soliton structure" is not clearly supported by the static strip; the difference is subtle. → suggest: annotate frames with total-mass values to reinforce the mass-conservation claim, or note the t=255 baseline edge-drift explicitly.

**[FIGURE NOTE] narrative.png: using illustrative parameters (μ=0.17, α=1.3), not the actual CMA-ES best_params** — log.md acknowledges this, but the figure title says "the exact input to the judge" which is misleading since the judge receives the search-optimized params, not these hand-picked ones. → suggest: retitle to "illustrative rollout" or regenerate after the Modal run with actual best_params.

---

### schematic.png



**[FIGURE ISSUE] schematic.png: large whitespace dead zones top and bottom** — the figure has approximately 40% blank vertical space. The diagram content occupies only the middle band. → suggest: reduce `figsize` height from 3.6 to ~2.2 or shift the content to use more of the canvas.

**[FIGURE ISSUE] schematic.png: the blue feedback arc arrow points to A_t but emerges near U=K*A** — the conservation arc should visually close the loop from A_{t+1} back to A_t; the current `connectionstyle="arc3,rad=-0.25"` produces a curve whose tail starts near the second node rather than cleanly from the last node. → suggest: adjust arc endpoints to connect exactly from A_{t+1} x-coordinate to A_t x-coordinate.

**[FIGURE NOTE] schematic.png: uses LaTeX-style notation in plain text (A_t, A_{t+1})** — this renders as literal underscore text rather than subscripts. Acceptable given matplotlib text mode, but inconsistent with the log.md's proper LaTeX equations.

---

### results.png



**[FIGURE ISSUE] results.png: all 4 restart curves are monotonically increasing straight lines** — the synthetic search trace uses `np.maximum.accumulate` on a random walk, producing perfectly smooth ramps. Real CMA-ES traces show plateaus and occasional drops in per-generation best. The figure actively misleads about expected search dynamics. → suggest: add a note in the subplot title that this is synthetic, or use a more realistic generative model (step function with plateaus).

**[FIGURE ISSUE] results.png: y-axis on the left panel runs from −0.3 to +0.35 with no baseline reference line** — the actual CLIP-OE score range and its relationship to the baseline metric (0.1185) is not visible. → suggest: add a horizontal dashed line at `y=0` labeled "degeneracy boundary" or annotate the target OE range.

**[FIGURE ISSUE] results.png: bar chart on the right panel has no error bars or uncertainty indicators** — since these are synthetic values, no uncertainty is possible, but when regenerated from the real run the bars should show variance across seeds.

**[FIGURE LAYOUT] results.png: currently a 2-panel search-diagnostics figure** — consider extending to 3 panels (search trace | per-restart bars | frame strip of best rollout) when real Modal results are available, per style guide recommendation to combine into a multi-panel telling setup→result→comparison.

---

### behavior.gif

*GIF not inlined — cannot embed animated GIF in GitHub comment. See branch: `orbits/03-flow-lenia-clip-oe/figures/behavior.gif`*

**[FIGURE NOTE] behavior.gif: log.md description says "both curves are flat lines at Σ A ≈ 479.3 for all 256 steps"** — this is the key mass-conservation claim. The GIF's right panel mass-trace is the primary evidence. Cannot verify from static review; confirm the trace is visibly flat (< 1% deviation) in the rendered GIF.

---

## Quality Hints

**[QUALITY NOTE]** The Result section (`## Smoke evidence`) leads with code parse output rather than a scientific result. The log.md states "the metric in the frontmatter will be back-filled" — until that happens, there is no result to read in 3 sentences. The orbit is effectively pre-result; the log documents methodology only.

**[QUALITY NOTE]** Style guide says "Lead with the result, not the method" and "Keep it under 500 words unless warranted." The log.md is approximately 1,200 words of methodology before reaching smoke evidence. Consider condensing the `## Why Flow-Lenia should win tiers` and `## Search design` sections once the actual metric is known.

**[QUALITY NOTE]** `research/references/registry.yaml` does not exist — the log documents "best-effort; this orbit does not modify the registry." Consider either creating the file or removing the claim to avoid confusion for future orbits.

**[QUALITY NOTE]** `make_figures.py` contains a NumPy re-implementation of the Flow-Lenia rollout described as "bit-for-bit identical to research/eval/evaluator.py". This cannot be verified without running both; if there is numerical drift (float64 vs float32, different padding modes), the figures would show different dynamics than the judge actually sees.

---

## Synopsis

Orbit 03 is a pre-eval orbit: no Modal search run has completed and `metric: null` in the frontmatter, so no significance assessment is possible. The code correctly implements the documented 4-restart Sep-CMA-ES loop with CLIP-OE fitness and mass-conservation degeneracy penalty, with three minor doc/comment mismatches (docstring says "2-4 restarts", budget comment says "half" not 55%, `substrate_name` parameter is unused). Figures are illustrative-only using hand-picked parameters rather than search-optimized ones, and results.png uses synthetic monotone curves that misrepresent expected CMA-ES dynamics — both should be regenerated from the real Modal run before milestone review.

issue_comments.author: reviewer
issue_comments.body:
<!-- RE:REVIEW orbit=04-random-search-dense -->
**orbit-reviewer:** orbit/04-random-search-dense — Advisory Notes

---

## Significance

**verified_metric:** `null` — no Modal eval has been run. The `metric:` field in log.md frontmatter is explicitly `null`. There is no eval result to assess for significance. All significance fields below are set accordingly.

`[SIGNIFICANCE] metric=null (no Modal eval), δ=N/A → cannot assess vs threshold or 2σ — orbit is code+local-sanity only`

The local CPU sanity run produced a **CLIP-OE proxy** of `0.1605` with the pixel-novelty fallback path (not the full CLIP judge), which is not directly comparable to `lcf_judge_heldout`. The config baseline for random search is `0.15` (`random_search_30min`). No Modal judge run has been submitted.

---

## Code Quality

**Overall: clean with minor notes.**

The prose description in log.md and the code in solution.py are well-aligned. The three OOM suspects (XLA retrace, CLIP buffer, list retention) are addressed in code as described. Specific checks:

- Proposal distribution matches log.md: 60% narrow Gaussian (std=0.35), 30% uniform, 10% wide Gaussian (std=1.2). Matches table in log exactly.
- Fixed-shape JIT cache via `_ROLLOUT_CACHE["rollout"]` is correct — compiled once at module load, never re-entered.
- `block_until_ready()` + `del traj` before next iteration — matches claim of no list-retention.
- `gc.collect()` every 100 candidates — matches log.

`[CODE NOTE]` The rollout function has a `jnp.concatenate` branch that zero-pads if `params` has fewer than 8 elements. However, `_DIM["lenia"] = 8` and the proposal always emits exactly 8-dim arrays. The dead branch is harmless but adds confusion — the `jnp.zeros(max(0, 8 - jnp.asarray(params).size), ...)` expression is also evaluated eagerly inside a JIT context on every call (the `max(0, ...)` is a Python-level expression that computes from concrete array size at trace time). This is safe but non-obvious.

`[CODE NOTE]` The `search()` signature accepts `seed_pool_train: jax.Array` but only uses it as integer seeds (`int(seed_pool_train[c % n_train])`). This is correct — the evaluator passes a 1D integer array of held-out seeds. Alignment with the evaluator contract is good.

`[CODE NOTE]` The archive logic only appends when `score > best_score` (strictly improving), so the "archive" is the sequence of best-so-far checkpoints, not a quality-diversity archive. The log.md prose says "top-K solutions for illumination" in the interface docstring comment in problem.md, but this orbit explicitly does not target QD. The archive is trimmed to last 16 entries. This is fine given the "control baseline" framing, but the field name `archive` may mislead the evaluator if it expects a population.

`[CODE NOTE]` `_WALL_SAFETY_S = 15.0` with a 1800 s budget → `safety_s = min(15, max(1, 180)) = 15`. This is the correct exit margin. But for very short smoke-test budgets (e.g., 10 s), the safety is `min(15, max(1, 1)) = 1 s`, so effectively 9 s of actual work in a 10 s budget. That is fine for local testing but worth noting.

`[CODE NOTE]` The comment inside `_clip_oe_single` says "CLIP is deterministic at temperature=0 (no sampling)" but CLIP image features involve no temperature parameter — the statement is vacuously true but misleading. The determinism guarantee actually comes from JAX's fixed PRNGKey seeding of the Lenia rollout (same `rollout_fn(params, train_seed)` call), not from CLIP itself.

---

## Reproducibility

`[REPRODUCIBILITY]` `metric: null` in frontmatter — no Modal eval has been run, so the metric is not recorded. This is documented intentionally in the log ("Running the 30-min Modal A100 eval is the campaign-orchestrator's job"), but it means this orbit cannot be compared quantitatively to others at milestone time without a separate eval run.

`[REPRODUCIBILITY]` `eval_version: eval-v1` is recorded in frontmatter — good.

Seed handling: The numpy RNG is derived deterministically from the JAX PRNGKey via `fold_in` → `randint`. The second run producing identical `best_params` confirms determinism. This is clean.

Hyperparameters are fully documented in both code constants and log.md table. No `TBD` or `...` gaps found.

---

## Figure Audit

### results.png



The figure is a 3-panel layout: (a) best-so-far convergence curve, (b) score density histogram, (c) text-card OOM-bypass claim summary.

`[FIGURE ISSUE] orbits/04-random-search-dense/figures/results.png: no panel labels (a)(b)(c) in the axes themselves — the suptitle references them but the axes lack the standard top-left corner labels as required by style.md → suggest: add ax.text(-0.12, 1.05, '(a)', transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=14, fontweight='bold') to each panel`

`[FIGURE ISSUE] orbits/04-random-search-dense/figures/results.png: panel (c) is a text card rendered as a matplotlib axis, not a data plot — this is a decorative/informational element that repeats content already in log.md. It contributes no data visualization → suggest: replace panel (c) with a 2D scatter of the 8-dim parameter space (first two PCA components colored by score) or a QQ-plot of score distribution; move the OOM-claim text into the log prose`

`[FIGURE ISSUE] orbits/04-random-search-dense/figures/results.png: panel (b) histogram uses default or narrow bins — the distribution appears to have very fine granularity around 0.04-0.08 with a long right tail. Use bins='fd' (Freedman-Diaconis) per style.md and add a KDE overlay to make the shape readable`

`[FIGURE NOTE]` The "trivial baseline (OE=0)" reference line in panel (a) is a useful anchor — this is good practice.

### narrative.png



The figure is a 2-row × 5-column grid comparing baseline p=0 (Orbium prior) vs. the random-search winner across 5 time steps.

`[FIGURE ISSUE] orbits/04-random-search-dense/figures/narrative.png: baseline row (top) shows the organism dying — frames t=63 through t=255 are entirely black (zero activity). While this accurately shows the baseline failure mode, it wastes 4 of 5 columns on empty frames. → suggest: replace t=127, t=191, t=255 for the baseline row with different seeds or different p=0 attempts, OR reduce to 3 frames (t=0, t=63, t=255) and use the freed space for a third row showing a second random-search candidate`

`[FIGURE ISSUE] orbits/04-random-search-dense/figures/narrative.png: no panel labels (a)(b)(c) — the two rows are not labeled with standard axis-level labels → suggest: add row labels as y-axis text or ax.text annotations per style.md`

`[FIGURE LAYOUT]` The behavior.gif is referenced in log.md but is a separate file. Per style.md ("one well-organized grid figure is better than 5 separate PNGs"), consider whether the gif's key frames could be folded into narrative.png as a third row.

### behavior.gif

Not directly readable as an image here (animated GIF), but its existence is noted. The log describes "24-frame loop of the best rollout, upscaled 3×" — this is appropriate for qualitative inspection.

`[FIGURE MISSING]` No illumination map / parameter-space coverage plot. Given that 2466 candidates were evaluated in 8-dim space, a PCA or UMAP projection of the candidate parameters colored by score would show whether the mixture proposal actually covers the viable shell vs. concentrating near p=0. This is the key visualization for validating the proposal distribution design.

---

## Quality Hints

`[QUALITY NOTE]` The log.md "Result" section is embedded in the "Local sanity run" table rather than a dedicated `## Result` heading. The punchline (zero RSS growth, OOM-bypass hypothesis validated, best proxy=0.161) must be inferred from the table and surrounding prose. Per style.md guidance to "lead with the result, not the method" and make the main result graspable in 3 sentences, a dedicated `## Result` section would help campaign-reviewer scan quickly.

`[QUALITY NOTE]` References section is present with URLs — ASAL and Lenia papers both have arxiv links. However, there is no `research/references/registry.yaml` file in the repo, so the check for registry membership cannot be performed. If this file is expected by the campaign, it should be created and these references added.

`[QUALITY NOTE]` The `## Prior Art & Novelty` section is present and well-hedged ("No novelty claim on the search algorithm itself. Random search is the control."). This is exemplary honest positioning.

`[QUALITY NOTE]` The `## Glossary` section is present and complete. All uppercase acronyms found in the log (ASAL, CLIP-OE, CMA-ES, ES, FM, JIT, OOM, OE, RSS, VLM) are defined. No undefined abbreviations found.

`[QUALITY NOTE]` The log claims the orbit is "code + local-sanity evidence + figures" as its deliverable, explicitly deferring the Modal eval to the orchestrator. This is a legitimate split of responsibility, but it means the orbit has no quantitative result to contribute to the campaign leaderboard. Campaign-reviewer should decide whether to trigger the Modal eval or treat this orbit as infrastructure/control only.

---

## Synopsis

Orbit 04-random-search-dense is an engineering control orbit, not a method contribution: it demonstrates that a stateless fixed-shape JIT random search avoids the XLA-retrace OOM that crashed Sep-CMA-ES at 30 min, validated by a local CPU sanity run showing zero RSS growth across 2466 candidates. The code is well-aligned with the log description and the reproducibility story is solid, but the orbit has no quantitative Modal eval result (`metric: null`) and therefore cannot be placed on the campaign leaderboard until the orchestrator triggers the 30-min A100 run. The figures are functional but would benefit from panel labels, removal of the decorative text-card panel in results.png, and addition of a parameter-space coverage plot showing the mixture proposal's distribution across the 8-dim viable shell.

<!-- RE:CRITIC -->

**Verdict: ACCEPT** (with MINOR issues noted for future improvement)

Overall: Four competent figures that tell a coherent story — baseline is a dead blob, DINOv2-OE best evolves structured Lenia patterns, search trace shows clean convergence to -0.9424, and the supplementary oe_comparison.png justifies why the DINOv2-lite inner loop is a meaningful replacement for CLIP-like. Resolutions, file sizes, typography, and palette all meet the style guide.

```json
{
  "verdict": "ACCEPT",
  "overall_comment": "Four figures read cleanly and tell a coherent baseline-vs-method story; only minor polish items (missing metric annotation on narrative + gif, unexplained timing spikes, single-seed).",
  "figures": [
    {
      "path": "orbits/05-dinov2-oe/figures/narrative.png",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Final metric value (-0.9424 DINOv2-lite OE) is not annotated anywhere on the figure", "fix": "add the best-score number to the second row title, e.g. 'DINOv2-OE best  |  DINOv2-lite OE = -0.9424  (Sep-CMA-ES + DINOv2-lite-OE inner loop)'"},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Baseline row repeats the same frame 4 times without explaining why (theta=0 produces no dynamics)", "fix": "add a small subtitle under the baseline row 'state identical across t — no evolution' so the reader isn't confused into thinking it's a rendering bug"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "path": "orbits/05-dinov2-oe/figures/results.png",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "No variance/error bars — single-seed trace only", "fix": "if seed budget allows, add 3-seed shaded confidence band to panel (a); otherwise explicitly label 'n=1 seed' in the suptitle"},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Panel (b) shows large periodic spikes (~every 10 gens) with no explanation; a reader will wonder if it's a bug", "fix": "annotate one spike with 'periodic JIT cache clear / gc' or similar, or add a one-line caption under panel (b)"},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "Best-so-far metric value -0.9424 not called out on panel (a)", "fix": "add a horizontal dashed line or text annotation 'final best = -0.9424' at the right edge of panel (a)"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "path": "orbits/05-dinov2-oe/figures/behavior.gif",
      "issues": [
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "First (poster) frame is raw initial noise — a GitHub reader who doesn't click sees nothing of the evolved pattern", "fix": "either start the gif a few steps in so the poster frame already shows structure, or composite a small caption 'Lenia rollout (t=0..255), DINOv2-OE best, score -0.9424' onto every frame"},
        {"severity": "MINOR", "issue": "No textual context on the gif itself", "fix": "burn a one-line caption with method name and metric into the frames using PIL before saving"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "path": "orbits/05-dinov2-oe/figures/oe_comparison.png",
      "issues": []
    }
  ]
}
```

All figures accepted as publish-worthy. The minors above can be addressed in a follow-up polish pass but are not blocking.

issue_comments.body:
<!-- RE:EVAL orbit=05-dinov2-oe -->
## Eval Check (batch 1, seed 1)
**Result:** VERIFIED (beats baseline — top of leaderboard)
**Measured:** 0.293939
**Seeds:** 1/16 held-out (full 16-seed run deferred to next batch)
**Wall clock:** 1533.8s
**Evaluator:** eval-v1
**Baseline anchor:** LCF_judge=0.1185 (METRIC=0 by construction)

**Branch:** [orbit/05-dinov2-oe](https://github.com/canele-ai/artificial-life2/tree/orbit/05-dinov2-oe)
**Solution:** [solution.py](https://github.com/canele-ai/artificial-life2/blob/orbit/05-dinov2-oe/orbits/05-dinov2-oe/solution.py)
